description: UE5 USTRUCT and UENUM: declaring structs and enums for Blueprint exposure, serialization, replication, and data tables

entries.title: USTRUCT — Custom Structs
entries.description: USTRUCT declares a C++ struct visible to UE5's reflection system. Structs are value types used for grouping related data, DataTable rows, function parameters, and replicated data.
entries.whatItIs: A USTRUCT is a plain-old-data container with UE5 reflection support. Unlike UObjects, structs are value types (copied, not referenced), have no garbage collection overhead, and are ideal for lightweight data bundles passed between systems.
whenToUse: Grouping related data fields together — FWeaponStats, FInventoryItem, FQuestData
DataTable row definitions — must inherit FTableRowBase
Function parameters when you need to pass multiple values — cleaner than 5+ parameters
Replicated data packets — structs can be replicated efficiently
Anywhere you need a lightweight data bundle without the overhead of a UObject
whenNotToUse: When you need garbage collection, polymorphism, or Blueprint subclassing — use UObject
When the data needs its own lifecycle (BeginPlay, Tick) — use UActorComponent
When you need inheritance hierarchies with virtual functions — structs support limited inheritance but no virtual dispatch
When the struct would hold UObject pointers that need GC tracking — use UPROPERTY on the struct fields
commonMistakes: Forgetting GENERATED_BODY() in the struct — causes cryptic compilation errors
Not using UPROPERTY() on struct fields — they won't show in the editor, won't serialize, and won't replicate
Comparing structs with == without defining operator== — UE5 does not auto-generate it for USTRUCTs
Using raw UObject* pointers in structs without UPROPERTY — GC won't track them and the pointer will dangle
Not marking the struct as BlueprintType when it needs to be used in Blueprints
Inheriting from FTableRowBase for all structs — only needed for DataTable rows
keyPoints: USTRUCT(BlueprintType): makes the struct usable in Blueprints
GENERATED_BODY(): required macro inside every USTRUCT
UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere, BlueprintReadWrite): expose fields to editor and Blueprint
Can have UFUNCTION() member functions if the struct is marked BlueprintType
Structs are value types — assigning copies the data, not a reference
NetSerialize(): override for custom network serialization (optimization for replication)
FTableRowBase: inherit from this for DataTable row structs
Constructor: provide defaults in the constructor or with field initializers

entries.title: UENUM — Custom Enums
entries.description: UENUM declares a C++ enum visible to UE5's reflection system. Enums define named constants for states, types, categories — used in switch statements, Blueprint Select nodes, and UPROPERTY dropdowns.
entries.whatItIs: A UENUM is a reflected C++ enum (typically enum class) that integrates with UE5's editor, Blueprints, and serialization. UMETA tags provide display names and tooltips for each value.
whenToUse: Defining discrete states — ECharacterState::Idle, Running, Attacking, Dead
UPROPERTY dropdown selection — designers pick from a list in the editor
Blueprint Switch nodes — clean branching based on enum values
Replicated state that maps to a small set of named values
whenNotToUse: When you need a hierarchical or composable categorization — use Gameplay Tags instead
When the set of values changes at runtime or is data-driven — use FName or FGameplayTag
When you need more than ~50 values — enums get unwieldy; consider a DataTable lookup
When the values need associated data (name, icon, stats) — use an enum + DataTable or DataAsset
commonMistakes: Using plain enum instead of enum class — plain enums pollute the namespace and can cause name collisions
Forgetting BlueprintType — the enum won't be usable in Blueprints
Not specifying uint8 as the underlying type — UE5 UPROPERTYs with enums require uint8 for Blueprint compatibility
Adding/removing/reordering enum values in a shipped game — breaks serialized save data. Add new values at the end.
Not providing UMETA(DisplayName) — the editor shows the raw C++ name which may be unclear to designers
keyPoints: UENUM(BlueprintType): makes the enum usable in Blueprints
enum class EMyEnum : uint8 — always use enum class with uint8 for Blueprint compatibility
UMETA(DisplayName="Friendly Name"): sets the name shown in editor dropdowns
UMETA(Hidden): hides a value from Blueprint dropdowns (for internal-only values)
Use in UPROPERTY: UPROPERTY(EditAnywhere) EMyEnum MyValue;
Switch on enum: switch(MyValue) { case EMyEnum::Value1: ... }
UEnum::GetValueAsString(): convert enum to string for logging
MAX value convention: add a _MAX entry at the end for iteration/count
